filename: HCL Proposal for POC of Creo-Catia on AWS Cloud for SBD.pptx

decomposition.title: Proposal for POC of Creo & Catia on AWS Cloud

requirements.text: Feasibility of running Creo 4.0, CATIA, and Windchill Workgroup Manager on AWS
Perform performance and usability testing of CAD applications (Creo, CATIA) on AWS
Integrate with on-prem Windchill server to validate check-in/check-out, upload on save, and workspace actions
Assess Creo-Catia-Windchill integrations and data flow for cloud deployment
Provision AWS environment (US East region) and deploy CAD applications in AWS AppStream
requirements.jurisdiction_note: Data hosted in AWS US East region

requirements.text: Provide weekly review updates and final management review/signoff with SBD stakeholders
Ensure availability of Creo & Catia licenses on AWS for POC usage
Provide test cases for validation of CAD apps and document test results for final comparison report

ambiguities: Unclear responsibility for AWS landing zone setup and network connectivity.
Unclear whether vendor or SBD provides Creo & Catia license for AWS.

win_intel.win_probability_rationale: No direct reference deals of this exact scope or technology (Creo/CATIA on AWS) exist in past proposals or the deal corpus. While we have broader manufacturing experience, the lack of documented POC-level credentials in CAD SaaS integrations lowers expected competitiveness.
capability_gaps: No proven track record with short-term POC for Creo/CATIA on AWS
Lack of references demonstrating prior integration with on-prem Windchill in a cloud context
No direct evidence of pre-existing specialized accelerators or IP specific to PTC/Dassault solutions
recommended_win_themes: Emphasize domain expertise from larger manufacturing transformations, highlighting applicability to this smaller-scale POC
Underscore rapid mobilization capability and quick provisioning of AWS AppStream environments
Demonstrate strong compliance approach and knowledge of AWS architecture best practices, even for short project durations
Highlight partnership potential or existing relationships with AWS and relevant CAD vendors to alleviate concerns about licensing or support
risk_factors: Client uncertainty about our lack of direct POC references for CAD integrations
Potential licensing and technical dependencies if PTC or Dassault conditions are not clarified
Short timeline demands accelerated setup and proof of performance
must_fix_before_bidding: Secure or confirm availability of necessary CAD licenses for AWS usage
Obtain client or partner references for PTC or Dassault solutions to bolster credibility
Clarify technical approach for Windchill on-prem integration and set up a demonstration if possible

cto_stated_priorities: Establish technical feasibility of running Creo, CATIA, and Windchill integrations in AWS
Validate performance and usability of CAD apps in a cloud environment
Ensure seamless data flow between AWS-hosted apps and on-prem Windchill
Deliver timely stakeholder reviews and final signoff
cfo_budget_signals: Likely focused on minimal spend for initial proof-of-concept
Short POC timeframe and relatively small budget ($30k) indicate controlled spending for quick feasibility validation
technology_debt_signals: Integration with on-prem Windchill signals reliance on legacy infrastructure
Desire to shift CAD workflows to the cloud suggests frustrations with on-prem performance or scalability
recent_strategic_moves: Initiating a cloud-based CAD proof-of-concept to explore modernization of engineering workflows
Expanding interest in AWS AppStream for potential enterprise-wide CAD deployments post-POC
unstated_needs: RFP is ambiguous about whether vendor or client sets up AWS networking → unstated need: vendor-led AWS landing zone setup to ensure connectivity
RFP repeatedly cites performance testing but never mentions security → unstated need: security and compliance architecture baked into the POC
RFP emphasizes weekly reviews and final signoff → unstated need: strong stakeholder management framework and smooth project governance
RFP does not clarify who provides CAD licenses → unstated need: clarity or flexible licensing approach to avoid POC delays

signals.signal: All requirements are labeled 'technical/must_have' with no exceptions or disclaimers
signals.implication: Scope is mandatory; the POC solution must fully address each requirement for success
Client demands rapid time-to-value and close coordination to confirm feasibility quickly

signals.signal: No mention of security or data protection in any requirement
signals.implication: They may assume security is handled; vendor must proactively address it to mitigate unnamed risk

signals.signal: Ambiguities on AWS network connectivity and licensing responsibilities
signals.implication: Client is uncertain about onsite vs. vendor roles; clarifying responsibilities could differentiate us

client_intel.recommended_narrative: We see a clear focus on validating CAD performance, usability, and integration in an AWS environment. SBD needs proof that cloud-based Creo and CATIA workflows can seamlessly integrate with on-prem Windchill. Addressing unspoken concerns around security, licensing, and networking setup will reassure them of the POC’s success. Emphasize rapid, well-structured delivery and strong governance (weekly updates, final signoff) to build trust, while providing clarity on security and licensing to minimize risk. Position this POC as the foundation for a potential broader rollout, highlighting our expertise in AWS best practices and legacy integration.

Underscore local US-based resources and faster response times.

recommended_differentiators: Show proven technical ability to integrate Creo and Catia with on-prem Windchill during short POCs.
Offer dedicated engineering SMEs with immediate AWS AppStream knowledge.
Provide a comprehensive test plan and performance metrics for cloud-based CAD usage.
competitor.price_to_win_range_usd: Likely around $25,000 - $30,000 (no competitor margin data available, low confidence).
competitor.killer_differentiator: A ready-to-deploy AWS-based CAD performance benchmarking lab specifically tailored for Creo and Catia, with integrated test suites, ensuring same-day usability results that none of the competitors can currently demonstrate based on the available evidence.

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: HCL would likely propose a standard AWS-based deployment for Creo, CATIA, and Windchill integrations, leveraging their engineering service experience. However, given no published references to PTC platforms on AWS, their detailed approach is unclear.

Potential focus on end-to-end cloud integration
ghost_bids.predicted_team_model: A small US-based project manager plus remote engineering/cloud specialists, scaled up from existing global delivery centers
key_vulnerabilities: No recent US-based POC references involving AWS for CAD apps (no public data)
No specialized job postings in the US indicating Creo/CATIA on AWS expertise
how_we_beat_this_bid: Highlight local credentials and proven references for PTC solutions on AWS
Emphasize immediate readiness for quick POC startup and specialized CAD domain expertise

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: Capgemini might push a digital transformation-driven approach to hosting Creo and CATIA on AWS. No specific references exist for CAD integrations with AWS, so the approach lacks confirmed details.

ghost_bids.likely_solution_approach: Deloitte would likely present a consulting-led strategy emphasizing enterprise transformation. With no documented experience specifically for Creo/CATIA on AWS, their approach remains high-level and consultative.

ghost_bids.overall_competitive_position: All four competitors appear to have general cloud integration capabilities but lack publicly confirmed experience with CAD-on-AWS POCs for Creo and CATIA. Each stands at a similar medium likelihood to bid without strong specialized references.
ghost_bids.single_biggest_risk: A competitor might underprice this 5-week POC to gain a foothold in future larger transformations, presenting a financially aggressive bid.
ghost_bids.recommended_counter_strategy: Highlight immediate test-ready AWS environment for Creo/CATIA, local resources to ensure quick ramp-up, and proven references. Emphasize domain expertise and the ability to deliver tangible performance metrics within the short POC window to outmaneuver generalist approaches.

deal_fingerprint.deal_archetype: Cloud Migration (Lift & Shift, Re-platform, Cloud-Native Rebuild)
deal_fingerprint.historical_win_rate_for_archetype: No relevant data found for small POCs
critical_success_factors: Ensure GPU-enabled AWS environment with robust performance for CAD apps
Validate on-prem Windchill integrations and secure connectivity
Coordinate properly licensed Creo and Catia usage in AWS
Complete thorough performance, functional, and user experience testing
Document test results in weekly reviews and final sign-off
common_failure_modes: Underestimating specialized CAD performance or GPU needs
Ambiguity over licensing ownership and provisioning
Insufficient clarity on network and landing zone setup
bid_conditions: Client must provide or confirm availability of Creo and Catia licenses for AWS
Client must clarify AWS landing zone and network connectivity responsibilities
Ensure pricing covers GPU-based AppStream environment costs

solution_options.name: Premium POC with Dedicated Onshore Team
solution_options.description: High-touch 5-week engagement with fully onshore CAD/AWS experts, advanced security and testing, ensuring minimal risk and maximum performance validation.
key_components: Fully onshore skilled CAD/AWS engineering resources
Enhanced performance and security testing framework
Dedicated project manager for weekly stakeholder reviews
Deep-dive assessment of Creo & Catia performance on AWS
solution_options.rationale: Provides the most comprehensive coverage and confidence through onshore resources and extended testing, but at a higher overall cost.

solution_options.name: Balanced POC (Recommended)
solution_options.description: Blended onshore/offshore delivery for the 5-week POC, including moderate security and performance tests, weekly reviews, and a balanced cost structure.
key_components: Combination of onshore solution architect and offshore engineering
Moderate security controls and performance benchmarking
Weekly checkpoints and final POC review with SBD stakeholders
AWS connectivity with on-prem Windchill verification
solution_options.rationale: Offers strong value at a competitive price point, leveraging a blended resource model and focusing on core POC requirements.

solution_options.name: Lean POC with Offshore Focus
solution_options.description: Cost-minimized approach utilizing mostly offshore resources, limited governance, and essential performance validation over 5 weeks.
key_components: Primarily offshore resources to reduce labor rates
Basic security checklist and performance tests
Reduced governance overhead and streamlined reporting
Core CAD integration tests against on-prem Windchill
solution_options.rationale: Minimizes overall cost but increases delivery risk due to heavier reliance on offshore teams and reduced governance.

pricing.competitive_rationale: Competitors are expected to come in around $25K-$30K. Option B is a strong balance of affordability, scope coverage, and timely results, enhancing win likelihood.

pursuit_plan.strategy: Focus on demonstrating rapid cloud deployment of Creo, CATIA, and Windchill integration. Emphasize technical expertise with AWS AppStream, CAD licensing, and end-to-end performance testing to assure SBD of the feasibility and value of cloud-based CAD environments.
pursuit_plan.win_strategy: Demonstrate a rapid and reliable CAD-on-cloud deployment approach—backed by proven technical expertise, thorough performance testing, and a clear plan for seamless integration with SBD’s on-prem Windchill environment.
pursuit_plan.pricing_approach: Adopt a cost-plus model for this short POC, factoring in AWS resource costs, potential licensing overhead, and a moderate margin to cover consulting services and demonstration of technical expertise.
key_risks: Ambiguities around AWS landing zone setup and network connectivity slowdown
Lack of clear licensing ownership could delay readiness for the POC
Performance or user-experience issues in real-time CAD usage on AWS

gaps: Limited competitor pricing intelligence—no direct cost data beyond general statements
No direct references for POC-level Creo/CATIA on AWS solutions from our side
strengths: Clear articulation of client’s unstated needs (security, networking, licensing)
Realistic assessment of capability gaps and potential mitigations
